narrative: The week unfolded in a stable Business as Usual regime with regime scores hovering between 21 and 24, indicating low systemic stress. High-yield spreads widened modestly from 276bp to 281bp—a 5bp move—reflecting gradual sentiment deterioration rather than acute dislocation. Two deep-value signals emerged late in the week (NEE on May 18 and BLK on May 19), both marked as WAIT, suggesting isolated pockets of repricing in mega-cap names without broader contagion to credit or equities. The regime remained internally consistent throughout, with no crossover into elevated stress, and the spread trajectory suggests investors are testing risk appetite at the margin rather than derisking systematically. Monitor whether the deep-value signals in quality names presage sector rotation or remain idiosyncratic, as a sustained uptick in such flags could signal the early phase of regime transition before it shows in headline spreads.
